description: Lexer tokenizes fn, if, else, for, in, break, <-, {, } keywords and delimiters
steps: Tokenize fn, if, else, for, in, break as keywords
Tokenize <- as TOKEN_RETURN
Tokenize { as TOKEN_LBRACE and } as TOKEN_RBRACE
Disambiguate < vs <- vs <=
Keywords do not collide with existing tokens or identifiers

description: Parser produces correct AST for fn decl, if/else, for/in, for loop, break, return, multi-assign, user-defined calls
steps: Parse fn name(params) { body } into FnDeclStmt
Parse if expr { body } into IfStmt
Parse if expr { body } else { body } into IfStmt with Else branch
Parse for ident in expr { body } into ForInStmt
Parse for { body } into ForLoopStmt (infinite loop)
Parse break into BreakStmt
Parse <- expr into ReturnStmt
Parse a, b = expr into MultiAssignStmt
Parse user-defined function calls as CallExpr (already exists)
Parse nested blocks (if inside fn, for inside if)
Parse fn with no params: fn foo() { body }
Parse empty body: fn foo() { }

description: Runtime supports iteration helpers for for-in loops
steps: Iterate() returns slice of single-element *SlopValue from parent array
Iterate on empty array returns empty slice
Iterate on nested array returns each element as *SlopValue

description: Codegen lowers FnDeclStmt, IfStmt, ForInStmt, ForLoopStmt, BreakStmt, ReturnStmt, MultiAssignStmt to Go AST
steps: Lower FnDeclStmt to *ast.FuncDecl with *SlopValue params and returns
Lower IfStmt to *ast.IfStmt with IsTruthy() condition
Lower IfStmt with else to *ast.IfStmt with Else block
Lower ForInStmt to *ast.RangeStmt over sloprt.Iterate()
Lower ForLoopStmt to *ast.ForStmt (infinite loop)
Lower BreakStmt to *ast.BranchStmt with token.BREAK
Lower ReturnStmt to *ast.ReturnStmt
Lower MultiAssignStmt to ast.AssignStmt with multiple LHS
Lower user-defined function calls (non-builtin CallExpr) to direct Go calls
Function declarations emitted before main()

description: 60+ e2e tests covering functions, if/else, for/in, infinite loops, break, return, multi-assign, nesting, edge cases
steps: fn decl + call: basic, no params, multi params, recursive
return: single value, early return, implicit empty return
if/else: truthy, falsy, else branch, no else, nested if
for/in: iterate array, empty array, single element, nested loop
infinite loop: immediate break, counter break, inside fn, accumulate, nested break
break in for-in: early exit from iteration
multi-assign: dual return from function
combined: fn with if inside, fn with for inside, for with if inside, fibonacci via loop
precedence: fn call in expression, fn call as argument
edge cases: fn calling fn, recursive fibonacci, fn with all statement types
fns.slop roadmap e2e test passes
